name: Autodesk Fusion 360 MCP Server
what_it_does: Autodesk Fusion 360 MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Fusion 360 — Autodesk's cloud-based CAD/CAM/CAE platform — creating 3D models and sketches, modifying component parameters, running simulations, generating toolpaths for manufacturing, and integrating AI-driven design automation into Fusion 360 mechanical engineering and product design workflows.
use_cases: Generating parametric 3D models from specifications from design automation agents
Modifying component dimensions and parameters from engineering optimization agents
Creating CAM toolpaths for CNC manufacturing from manufacturing agents
Running structural simulations and analyzing results from analysis agents
Automating repetitive CAD operations from productivity agents
Building AI-assisted mechanical design workflows from engineering agents
not_for: Teams not using Fusion 360 (use SolidWorks, CATIA, or FreeCAD MCPs)
Real-time simulation (CAE simulations have significant compute time)
best_when: A mechanical engineer or product designer using Fusion 360 needs AI-driven design automation — for parametric modeling, CAM toolpath generation, or repetitive design tasks.
avoid_when: You use SolidWorks, CATIA, or other CAD systems — Fusion 360 API is specific to Autodesk's platform.

auth.notes: Fusion 360 must be running with add-in loaded. Autodesk account required. API access via Fusion 360 Python add-in framework.

pricing.notes: Fusion 360 requires Autodesk subscription for commercial use. Personal use is free. MCP server is free. Autodesk account always required.

performance.rate_limits: Local Fusion 360 — no API rate limits; CAD operation time varies by complexity

known_agent_gotchas: Fusion 360 must be running with Python add-in active — cannot automate without open application
Fusion 360 API changes frequently with product updates — MCP compatibility may break
CAD operations can be slow for complex geometry — design agents for async patterns
Cloud documents require internet connectivity — offline CAD not supported
Community MCP — niche tool for Fusion 360 users; limited support
Autodesk account required even for personal use — creates dependency on Autodesk infrastructure
agent_readiness.security_notes: Fusion 360 requires Autodesk account. CAD data stored in Autodesk cloud. Autodesk data processing policies apply to design files. Community MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: Autodesk cloud for design data. Account required. Design files in Autodesk infrastructure. Community MCP.
